articles.title: Are Prediction Markets Actually Good for Journalism?
articles.summary: Journalists and media critics are debating whether prediction markets help or hurt news reporting, as betting platforms increasingly influence how stories get covered and what gets attention. The outcome could determine whether newsrooms embrace prediction markets as reporting tools or dismiss them as gambling distractions that skew editorial priorities toward whatever drives the most action.

articles.title: Polymarket’s Fight Is for Next Generation of Financial Markets
articles.summary: Polymarket is positioning itself as the foundation for how financial markets will operate in the future, likely battling regulatory challenges and traditional finance incumbents. If they win this fight, prediction markets could become the standard way people price risk and discover information across all asset classes, not just political bets and crypto.

articles.title: Prediction Markets: The Emerging Regulatory Headache
articles.summary: Regulators are scrambling to figure out how to oversee prediction markets as they explode in popularity and real-world influence. The regulatory uncertainty could either legitimize the industry through clear rules or strangle it with compliance costs that favor deep-pocketed players over scrappy startups.

articles.title: Hundreds of millions wagered on Iran through online betting markets
articles.summary: Online bettors have wagered hundreds of millions of dollars on Iran-related outcomes, likely covering everything from military conflicts to regime change scenarios. This massive volume signals that geopolitical events are becoming major liquidity drivers for prediction markets, potentially rivaling traditional sports betting in scale and influence.

articles.title: In a vote of confidence for Meta’s Threads, Kalshi adds sharing feature
articles.summary: Kalshi just added a feature letting users share their trades directly to Meta's Threads social platform. The move signals Kalshi believes Threads has enough juice to drive meaningful engagement and new user acquisition for prediction markets.

articles.title: Live Results: Prediction Markets for GA-14 Special Election
articles.summary: Georgia's 14th congressional district is holding a special election and prediction markets are tracking the live results as votes come in. This race offers a real-time test of how quickly betting markets can process and price new information compared to traditional polling and media coverage.

articles.title: Is Kalshi vs Polymarket the next OpenAI vs. Anthropic? What to know about the newest fintech rivalry
articles.summary: Two US-based prediction market platforms are squaring off in what could become tech's next major corporate rivalry, with Kalshi and Polymarket competing for dominance in the rapidly growing political betting space. The winner will likely shape how Americans bet on elections and news events for years to come, potentially determining whether prediction markets become mainstream or remain niche.

articles.title: Polymarket Calls In Palantir to Be the Adult in the Room
articles.summary: Polymarket has brought in Palantir, the data analytics giant, presumably to handle compliance, risk management, or regulatory oversight as the platform faces increased scrutiny. This signals Polymarket is trying to professionalize its operations ahead of potential regulatory crackdowns, which could either legitimize prediction markets or saddle them with expensive compliance costs that kill the fun.

articles.title: Polymarket Taps Palantir, TWG AI to Police Sports Bets
articles.summary: Polymarket is bringing in Palantir and TWG AI to help monitor and regulate sports betting activity on its platform. This signals the prediction market is getting serious about compliance ahead of potential regulatory scrutiny, which could either legitimize the space or create new barriers for retail bettors.

articles.title: Inflation outlook: Prediction markets see rising odds of a hotter CPI print
articles.summary: Prediction markets are pricing in higher chances that the next Consumer Price Index report will come in above expectations, signaling traders expect inflation to run hotter than the Fed wants. If CPI does spike, it kills hopes for aggressive rate cuts and sends shockwaves through markets betting on everything from Treasury yields to recession timing.

articles.title: This 29‑Year‑Old Founder Took On a Federal Regulator to Scale Her Company—and Built an $11 Billion Giant
articles.summary: A 29-year-old founder battled federal regulators to grow her startup into an $11 billion company, proving that regulatory friction doesn't always kill ambitious businesses. Her playbook for navigating compliance while scaling fast offers a template that prediction market platforms desperately need as they face their own regulatory gauntlet.

articles.title: Gamblers can now bet on the outcome of wars – and that’s a problem
articles.summary: Betting platforms are now accepting wagers on military conflicts and war outcomes, turning geopolitical violence into gambling fodder. This crosses a moral line that could trigger regulatory crackdowns on prediction markets just as they're gaining mainstream legitimacy.
